items.title: Stop Overpaying for EBS I The Keys to AWS Optimization | S16 E3

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this livestream is a practical deep dive into Amazon EBS cost optimization, showing how to find overprovisioned volumes, right-size storage, and leverage newer EBS volume types to better match performance with cost. It also covers eliminating waste from unused snapshots, outdated volumes, and inefficient configurations, with real-world examples and monitoring tools to cut EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
bullets: Identify and right-size overprovisioned EBS volumes.
Leverage latest EBS volume types to align performance and cost.
Eliminate waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes.
Demonstrations of monitoring tools for storage utilization.
Real-world examples and actionable strategies to reduce EBS spend.
takeaways: Reducing EBS overprovisioning and removing stale snapshots directly lowers recurring cloud storage costs.
Choosing the right EBS volume type improves performance-per-dollar, preventing unnecessary spend for workloads.
Continuous monitoring and governance (DevOps/FinOps) are needed to sustain storage cost savings.
Actionable cleanup and right-sizing steps can cut waste without degrading application performance.

items.title: AWS Power Hour: Generative AI For Developers | S1 E1 | Exam Overview and FM Integrations

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This episode breaks down the exam format and study strategies while guiding listeners through designing resilient generative AI architectures. It covers building data processing pipelines for foundation models, implementing vector stores for RAG systems, applying prompt engineering strategies, and analyzing sample exam questions with real-world solutions.
bullets: Overview of exam format and study strategies for GenAI-related certification.
Guidance on designing resilient generative AI architectures.
How to build data processing pipelines for foundation models.
Implementing vector stores for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems.
Prompt engineering techniques and analysis of sample exam questions.
takeaways: Plan infrastructure capacity and resilience to support production-grade GenAI architectures described in the episode.
Provision high-throughput data pipelines and storage to handle foundation model input/output and preprocessing needs.
Ensure low-latency storage and indexing support for vector stores used in RAG workflows.
Invest in staff skills and operational practices (including prompt engineering) needed to deploy and maintain advanced GenAI systems.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York intending to build Airbnb properties and spent the next three years commuting from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself. He added more cabins over time, and as of September 2025 the property has generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015: Chris Broomfield purchased a five-acre property in upstate New York to develop Airbnb rentals.
Over the next three years he made weekend commutes from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin by himself.
Additional cabins were built on the property after the initial A-frame.
As of September 2025 the property has brought in over $2.1 million in revenue.
takeaways: Concentrated short-term rental development can create localized demand spikes for reliable power and broadband—plan capacity and resilience accordingly.
Small land investments that scale into multiple units may lead to clustered load growth in rural areas served by a single feeder or substation.
Remotely managed, self-built properties suggest value in remote monitoring, automated controls, and backup power to minimize on-site maintenance and downtime.
Phased development over years implies opportunities for modular, scalable infrastructure deployments rather than large upfront upgrades.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the podcast discusses how to view operational changes (like moving production, distribution, teams) through the lens of transfer pricing and valuations, with guidance on recognizing significant restructurings and analysing EBIT. It highlights reporting obligations (TPR, MDR), the importance of timely documentation, common valuation mistakes, practical examples (production/distribution moves, employee transfers, contract transfers) and ends with a checklist of actions for firms.
bullets: Definition and recognition of restructuring from a transfer pricing perspective.
How to identify material changes and analyse EBIT impact.
Importance of preparing restructuring documentation in a timely manner.
Relevant reporting obligations: TPR and MDR.
Role of valuations, frequent errors, practical examples and a closing checklist.
takeaways: Treat operational moves (production/distribution shifts, workforce or contract transfers) as potential restructurings for TP and valuation review.
Assess EBIT impact early to determine materiality and reporting needs (TPR/MDR).
Prepare and maintain documentation on time to reduce future compliance risk and stress.
Engage valuation specialists early to avoid common valuation mistakes and pitfalls.
Use a structured checklist to ensure all reporting and documentation steps are covered.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta professional services, says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy rather than spending on compute. He advises businesses to reimagine value chains and deploy multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Most costly AI mistake: rushing to code without a strategy (not compute costs).
Escape the 'coding trap' by reimagining value chains.
Use multi-agent systems to drive tangible business outcomes.
Insight presented by Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (professional services).
takeaways: Prioritize strategic planning and value-chain design before scaling compute infrastructure.
Do not assume compute costs are the primary expense—focus on outcome-driven architecture and use cases.
Assess multi-agent architectures for coordinating complex workflows that deliver business value.
Consider engaging professional services (e.g., Google Delta) to translate strategy into implementation.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform that secures AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility and protection against evolving threats. It highlights AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing, and an AI Firewall for real-time prompt/response guardrails to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure.
bullets: Unified platform on Google Cloud to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens: visibility and policy application across user–AI interactions.
AI Red Teaming: stress-tests models and apps against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time protection of prompts and responses with built-in guardrails.
Designed to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Visibility is critical—discover and govern shadow AI before it creates risk.
Implement proactive red teaming to find vulnerabilities in models and applications.
Real-time guardrails (AI Firewall) help prevent data loss and policy violations at the interaction layer.
Consider a cloud-native solution on Google Cloud to centralize AI security controls and reduce operational complexity.
